mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, the discount rate on future earnings declines and equity valuation multiples expand, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts then read as true easing rather than panic.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in most periods when FEDFUNDS is declining year-over-year, or if the signal loses directional power once CPIAUCSL inflation is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks labor-market deterioration and weaker consumer income growth, which should reduce earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if PAYEMS does not deteriorate alongside the unemployment rise.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in retail sales relative to housing activity signals resilient consumer demand without an immediate housing overhang, supporting near-term revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Reject if the spread between RSAFS growth and HOUST growth does not improve hit rates versus either series alone, or if positive spreads are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO after a prior trough indicates industrial reacceleration and better capacity utilization, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if a 3-month rise in INDPRO is not associated with positive 60-day forward SPX returns, or if the relationship reverses during recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deterioration in housing starts is an early demand shock because housing feeds construction, credit, and durable-goods demand; when HOUST falls below trend, S&P 500 returns over the next month turn negative.
failure_condition: Reject if deep HOUST contractions are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the hit rate is indistinguishable from unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration coupled with a still-elevated policy rate tightens real financial conditions, compresses valuation multiples, and should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-policy-rate episodes do not lead to negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction adds no predictive value beyond CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is losing jobs and slack is increasing simultaneously, which signals weaker household income and earnings pressure. That combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll declines and unemployment increases do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if either labor series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A broad sales slowdown measured by RSAFS, when paired with falling GDPC1 growth, indicates weakening final demand across consumers and firms. That should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in RSAFS and GDPC1 does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once either series is removed from the interaction.
